description:
Offered: every other year
This course will cover United States history from 1945 to 1968 and focus on developments in politics, culture, international affairs, and the economy. Topics will include the rise of suburbia, the politics of the welfare state, the grassroots civil rights movement and feminism, and the influence of Cold War tensions, television, and economic prosperity on American life.
Prerequisite(s): AMST 1800 or ENGL 1200 or HIST 1000 or HIST 1100 or HIST 1400 or HIST 1500
Meets General Education Requirement(s): AIA, CV, HI
prerequisites: AMST 1800 or ENGL 1200 or HIST 1000 or HIST 1100 or HIST 1400 or HIST 1500
